title: qa_regression_filed event payload
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-23 in WU 3.4 as the QA regression first-failure signal. Emitted by the qa-regression skill on first `qa_execution_failed` for a given `(implementation_task_correlation_id, test_id)` pair, after the skill has written the inbox artifact at `regression_inbox_file`. Pairs with qa_regression_resolved.schema.json. Idempotence key is `(implementation_task_correlation_id, test_id)`: the skill refuses to file a second artifact for the same pair (repeat failures escalate `spinning_detected` on the original implementation task instead). The `implementation_task_correlation_id` is resolved from the feature task graph (SOT = feature registry), per WU 3.3 D3.
description: Payload shape for the `qa_regression_filed` event emitted by the QA agent when it files a regression inbox artifact in response to a `qa_execution_failed`. The envelope `correlation_id` is feature-level; the payload identifies the original implementation task the regression is attributed against, the specific failing `test_id`, the backlink to the failing qa-execution event, and the path to the inbox artifact written under `/inbox/qa-regression/`. The inbox artifact is the substrate that spawns a NEW `implementation` task against the component repo under test; the QA agent does not mint that task's correlation ID (the PM inbox consumer or the human does), so this payload carries no `spawned_implementation_task_correlation_id` field — detection of a linked fix attempt in downstream qa-regression passes relies on the event log, not on this payload (see agents/qa/skills/qa-regression/SKILL.md).

implementation_task_correlation_id.description: Task-level correlation ID of the ORIGINAL implementation task the regression is attributed against — the task whose delivered code failed a qa-execution run. Resolved by the qa-regression skill from the feature task graph by intersecting the qa_execution task's `depends_on` with implementation-type tasks (Q4 algorithm in the skill). Together with `test_id`, forms the regression idempotence key.

test_id.description: The kebab-case `test_id` of the failing test, matching the `test_id` field in the plan file's `tests[]` entry and in the `failed_tests[].test_id` field of the upstream `qa_execution_failed` event. Stability is load-bearing: renames would orphan open regressions (see agents/qa/skills/qa-authoring/SKILL.md §"Step 5").

failing_qa_execution_event_ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the upstream `qa_execution_failed` event this regression is filed in response to. Backlink into the feature event log; paired with this event's own `correlation_id` (feature-level) a consumer can locate the original failure signal.

failing_commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA (40-char full hex) against which the failing qa-execution ran — i.e., the `commit_sha` field from the upstream `qa_execution_failed` payload. Carried here for event-log self-containment: a reader resolving the regression without opening the plan file or the upstream event can still pin the commit under regression.

regression_inbox_file.description: Path to the inbox artifact written under `/inbox/qa-regression/`, relative to the orchestration repo root (e.g. `inbox/qa-regression/FEAT-2026-0061-widgets-export-rate-limit-enforced-429.md`). The artifact's frontmatter+body is the substrate that spawns a new implementation task against the component repo under test; the spawn itself is performed by the PM inbox consumer or the human, not by this skill.
